title: Cup Anemometer — Wind Speed Measurement
content: A three-cup anemometer converts wind speed to a rotational frequency. Wind speed is proportional to rotation rate: V = k · f, where k is the instrument calibration factor and f is the rotation frequency.
extended_content: Typical calibration factors: k ≈ 0.75–1.0 m/s per Hz for a standard 100 mm cup set.  A reed switch or Hall-effect sensor generates one or more pulses per revolution, counted by a microcontroller.  Starting threshold ('threshold wind speed') is typically 0.3–0.5 m/s.  WMO specification requires measurement range 0.2–75 m/s with ±0.5 m/s accuracy below 5 m/s and ±10% above.  Ultrasonic anemometers have no moving parts and better low-wind response.
source: WMO-No.8 Guide to Instruments, Ch.5; R.M. Young 05103 Manual
